description: #582/#585 asymmetric/spatial-isolated 3D CR3BP niching-GA search re-run at the ACTUAL Earth-Moon mass ratio (mu=1.2150584270572e-2), vs. #582/#585's mu=0.001 generic Sun-planet system. Symmetry-breaking s=0.15 (the #585-certified rung at mu=0.001); paper-scale GA budget (pop=200, gen=400) per MMR via search/niching_ga.py's deterministic-crowding niching layer, unmodified except MU.

mmrs_excluded.3:2: Positive control at this mu FAILED severely (x0_err=69.6%, ydot0_err=71.1%, T_err=22.1%; corrector did not converge, residual 1.9e-1). The seed's own osculating mean-motion ratio was already anomalous at generation (n_ratio=2.02 vs the expected 1.5 for 3:2), consistent with a genuine family-selection breakdown -- 3:2's a1=0.763 sits closest to the secondary of all 5 tabulated interior MMRs, and at mu=0.0122 (12x mu=0.001) the mu-independent linearized seed guess is no longer close enough to a true periodic orbit for the small-box corrector to reliably recover it. Also run at paper scale for completeness/transparency (0/25 clusters converged, confirming the diagnosis) but its results are NOT trusted and are excluded from any novelty claim.

positive_controls.4:1: PASS (x0=0.70%, ydot0=0.40%, T=0.30%, jacobi_abs=2.81e-3; all well inside tolerance) -- PRIMARY trust anchor for this mu.
positive_controls.5:2: marginal FAIL at small-GA-budget (pop=40,gen=60): corrector converged (residual 6.3e-12) but landed on a nearby distinct symmetric member outside tight tolerance (x0=4.0%, T=10.5%, jacobi_abs=5.35e-2) -- basin/continuum competition, not non-convergence.
positive_controls.3:1: marginal FAIL at small-GA-budget: corrector converged (residual 2.5e-12) but landed off-tolerance (x0=9.1%, T=9.0%, jacobi_abs=6.06e-2) -- same continuum-competition profile as 5:2.
positive_controls.5:1: marginal FAIL at small-GA-budget: corrector did NOT converge (residual 6.6e-2); x0=8.8%, ydot0=12.3% off.

positive_control_caveat: Only 4:1 passed cleanly under the reduced small-GA-budget positive-control harness (pop=40,gen=60, per script default). 5:2/3:1 converged to a nearby-but-distinct symmetric family member (plausible slow-convergence/continuum-competition at the reduced budget, not unlike the Gurfil-Kasdin 2002 F/N near-miss diagnosis in docs/notes/2026-07-12-581-niching-ga-stage2-positive-control.md); 5:1 did not converge at the reduced budget. All 3 were nonetheless included in the paper-scale (pop=200,gen=400) sweep below since (a) 4:1's clean pass demonstrates the underlying mechanism (fitness, corrector, GA) is sound at this mu, (b) the paper-scale budget is ~33x the small-budget effort and the paper-scale sweep itself is the actual reproducibility check for these 3 (all show the SAME symmetric-only outcome as 4:1, with 0 drift-to-neighboring-MMR across all 4 -- internally consistent), and (c) per this task's own instruction not to over-invest in validation. A stricter reviewer could ask for a bigger-budget positive control on 5:2/3:1/5:1 before fully trusting their individual results; flagged here rather than silently assumed.
literature_check_status: NOT RUN (structural matcher engagement confirmed only -- see literature_anchors_engaged per candidate). At the REAL Earth-Moon mu, the CandidateSignature's primary='Earth'/sequence=('Moon',) label (isolated_3d_asymmetric_pipeline.build_candidate_signature) is now a genuine physical claim, not #582's mu=0.001 'reuse fiction' -- so the matcher correctly pulls in real cislunar resonance literature (Braik-Ross 2026, Kumar-Rawat-Rosengren-Ross 2024/2026, Rawat-Kumar-Rosengren-Ross 2024/2025, Rosengren-Ross-Kumar-Rawat 2024, Hiraiwa et al. 2026, Sanaga-Park-Howell 2026, Antoniadou & Libert 2019) for every converged candidate below. This is a live-search adjudication step for a follow-up task, not this dispatch -- per [[feedback_literature_novelty_check_baseline]], not-found is necessary, not sufficient, for any novelty claim.
headline_result: 0/81 converged clusters (across the 4 trusted MMRs) classify asymmetric; every converged representative sits on a SYMMETRIC periodic orbit (perpendicular x-z-plane crossing), same qualitative outcome as #582 (0/104 at mu=0.001) and #585 (0/78 at mu=0.001, s=0.15). 0/81 drifted to a neighboring tabulated MMR. This extends the 'no asymmetric/spatial-isolated 3D family found by this method in this box' empty region from the generic mu=0.001 system to the actual Earth-Moon mass ratio -- a real physical system, not just a generic analog -- still METHOD-CONDITIONAL (same narrow, symmetric-seed-centered box; same t_frac=0.5 period-doubled-branch blind spot #585 already flagged). No candidate is structurally novel; nothing warrants a novel-orbit adjudication task. A literature-adjudication task on the converged symmetric members themselves (now matching REAL Earth-Moon literature, unlike the mu=0.001 fiction) could still be worthwhile as a corpus-coverage exercise, separate from any novelty claim.
